# Tech Lead Notes

> Multi-source, three-tier mirror of CC-licensed project-management resources. Tier 1 = agent index (compact catalog); Tier 2 = distilled per-concept cards (TL;DR / core ideas / anti-patterns / cross-refs); Tier 3 = full original text per source. License per source — see each chunk's `license:` frontmatter field.

- [Tech Lead Notes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/index.md): Operational playbook for tech leads — a multi-source, three-tier mirror of CC-licensed project-management, Scrum, and engineering- effectiveness resources, split into navigable chunks with YAML frontmatter so humans,…

## Tier 1 — Agent index

- [Agent index](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/agent-index.md): one-line catalog of every concept, principle, performance domain and methodology covered. Designed to fit in a system prompt; links out to Tier-2 cards.

## Tier 2 — Distilled cards

### Distilled — overview, glossary, cross-references

- [Distilled (Tier 2)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/index.md): This is a dense, agent-friendly distillation of Nader K. Rad's PMBOK Guide: Underneath the Surface (CC-BY-4.0). Anecdotes, historical commentary and personal asides are removed. Each card is a self-contained brief with…
- [About the distilled tier](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/about.md): The Tier-2 distilled cards (everything under docs/distilled/) are an editorial summary of Nader K. Rad's PMBOK Guide: Underneath the Surface. The original full text is preserved in Tier 3 (docs/01-…/ … docs/05-…/) and…
- [Cross-references](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/cross-references.md): A topic-to-source map. Use these clusters when you want to gather all the cards related to a single concept.
- [Glossary](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/glossary.md): Distilled definitions of recurring terms, derived from the book. Each entry links to the primary distilled card or full chunk that develops it.

### Distilled — PMBOK 7 principles

- [PMBOK 7 principles (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/index.md): Twelve principles that PMBOK 7 uses as a lens to interpret project management situations. They are not a methodology and not a checklist of activities — apply them to evaluate decisions and tailor methods.
- [Stewardship](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/01-stewardship.md): TL;DR: A project manager is a facilitator and steward of people and resources, not a technical decision-maker or a "boss".
- [Collaborative team environment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/02-collaborative-team.md): TL;DR: A great project needs a great team, not just great individuals. Build the team through real work, not corporate rituals.
- [Engage stakeholders](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/03-engage-stakeholders.md): TL;DR: A stakeholder is anyone with interest who can impact the project (not just the customer). Identify them aggressively, communicate often, and tailor the channel per group.
- [Focus on value](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/04-focus-on-value.md): TL;DR: Value is the benefits-to-cost ratio (not "benefits" alone). Every project decision must be traceable to why the project exists. Value lives at the portfolio layer; projects align to it and feed it information.
- [System interactions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/05-system-interactions.md): TL;DR: Decisions ripple through interconnected systems and are distorted by cognitive biases. Reason holistically and apply critical thinking, not gut feel.
- [Leadership behaviors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/06-leadership.md): TL;DR: Leadership is influencing others toward desired outcomes — a behavior, not a job title. Anyone can lead; PMs should both be leaders and find and empower hidden leaders.
- [Tailor based on context](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/07-tailor.md): TL;DR: No universal PM system fits every project. Tailor a chosen methodology to your size/culture/project — but tailoring is system design, not cherry-picking.
- [Build quality in](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/08-quality.md): TL;DR: Quality is prevention, integrated into the system, not final inspection bolted on. It applies to the product and to the PM system itself. Investment must be net-positive; if not, the quality system is poorly…
- [Optimize risk responses](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/09-risk.md): TL;DR: A risk is any uncertainty (positive or negative) that can impact the project. Manage risks proactively through layered responses, continuously, with cost-justified investments.
- [Navigate complexity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/10-complexity.md): TL;DR: Project complexity comes from three sources: people, non-linear interactions, and ambiguity/uncertainty. There is no distinct technique here — applying the other principles is how you navigate complexity.
- [Adaptability and resiliency](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/11-adaptability.md): TL;DR: Predictive vs adaptive (Agile) approaches each fit a different kind of product. PMBOK 7 is approach-agnostic for the product, but the PM system itself must always be adaptive because it deals with people.
- [Enable change to achieve the envisioned future state](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/pmbok-7/12-enable-change.md): TL;DR: Distinguish output (what the project builds) from outcome (what changes in the world). Outputs only matter when they cause adoption and outcomes; outcome management is largely program management, but the project…

### Distilled — NUPP principles

- [NUPP principles (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/index.md): NUPP = Nearly Universal Principles of Projects. Open non-proprietary resource (<https://nupp.guide>) — usable as a lens to re-interpret any other PM system. Six principles, intentionally generic and short.
- [Prefer results and the truth to affiliations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/01-results-over-affiliations.md): TL;DR: Don't be tribal about a methodology. Use what works for the project; learn from systems you don't even use.
- [Preserve and optimize energy and resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/02-preserve-energy.md): TL;DR: Project resources and the PM's decision-making energy are both finite. Optimize for high-leverage work and protect mental bandwidth.
- [Always be proactive](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/03-always-be-proactive.md): TL;DR: Reactive behavior is the default. Project work consistently benefits from proactive action — reach out before problems escalate.
- [A chain is only as strong as its weakest link](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/04-weakest-link.md): TL;DR: All PM domains interact. Over-investing in one (typically scheduling) while neglecting others ruins the lot.
- [Don't do anything without a clear purpose](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/05-clear-purpose.md): TL;DR: Every practice/artifact must have a purpose you can name. Otherwise it's cargo-cult work — looks like methodology, produces nothing.
- [Use repeatable elements](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/nupp/06-repeatable-elements.md): TL;DR: Ad-hoc work is expensive and error-prone. Build checklists and recurring cycles so cognition can go to the actual work.

### Distilled — PRINCE2 principles

- [PRINCE2 principles (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/index.md): Seven principles that support the PRINCE2 methodology. Mostly applicable to all projects, but designed as a coherent set with the PRINCE2 method (covered separately in Methodologies).
- [Continued business justification](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/01-continued-business-justification.md): TL;DR: Re-check the project's business case throughout — at predefined gates. Things change; sunk cost is not a reason to continue.
- [Learn from experience](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/02-learn-from-experience.md): TL;DR: Reusing existing PM systems and prior-project lessons is cheaper than reinventing them. Both between and within projects.
- [Defined roles and responsibilities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/03-roles.md): TL;DR: Beyond very small projects, people need explicit clarity on what's expected of them — and what they can expect from others.
- [Manage by stages](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/04-manage-by-stages.md): TL;DR: You can only plan in detail for a short horizon. Use a high-level plan up-front and a detailed plan only for the next stage.
- [Manage by exception](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/05-manage-by-exception.md): TL;DR: Delegate decisions within tolerances; escalate only when thresholds are breached. Saves senior energy and creates buy-in.
- [Focus on products](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/06-focus-on-products.md): TL;DR: Plan around the products (deliverables), not around activities. Activity-first planning prematurely commits to one solution.
- [Tailor to suit the project](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/principles/prince2/07-tailor-to-project.md): TL;DR: PRINCE2 (and almost every PM system) cannot be used out-of-the-box. Tailor up-front and keep adapting throughout.

### Distilled — performance domains

- [Performance domains (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/index.md): Eight domains PMBOK 7 uses to enrich any chosen methodology. They are sets of concerns to verify your methodology covers adequately for your project. If your method is thin in a domain, extend it (don't replace it).
- [Stakeholders (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/01-stakeholders.md): TL;DR: Five-step continuous loop — Identify → Understand & analyze → Prioritize → Engage → Monitor — covering both internal and external stakeholders. Modern approaches balance both; Agile-only or "external-only" focus…
- [Team (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/02-team.md): TL;DR: Three concerns: team structure, team building, and leadership. Two motivations: ethical (people deserve a safe healthy environment regardless of project gain) and instrumental (better team → better project).
- [Development approach and life cycle (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/03-development-approach.md): TL;DR: Predictive vs adaptive is decided by the product, not by team preference. "Hybrid" is mostly a misnomer; only valid form is multiple parallel projects with different approaches per independent product part.…
- [Planning (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/04-planning.md): TL;DR: All projects plan — adaptive ones plan differently, not less. Plan continually, cover all 10 knowledge areas, separate plan (artifacts) from meta-plan (how-to-plan).
- [Project Work (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/05-project-work.md): TL;DR: Plan first, then work — but with a constant two-way dance between plans and reality. Capture follow-up items as they emerge; never let them slip.
- [Measurement (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/06-measurement.md): TL;DR: Measure to direct work and to evaluate it. Use forecast to completion as the headline metric. What you measure becomes the goal — choose proxies that align with real value.
- [Delivery (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/07-delivery.md): TL;DR: Requirements → Deliverables → Activities is the correct order in both predictive and adaptive projects. Limit WIP. Verify each deliverable before delivery; never bet on a single big-bang final delivery.
- [Uncertainty (performance domain)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/performance-domains/08-uncertainty.md): TL;DR: Risk management is the dominant topic. Choose the sophistication that matches the project; default to simpler. Sensitive risks belong at the portfolio level, not project.

### Distilled — methodologies

- [Methodologies (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/index.md): The PMBOK Guide is not a methodology — it interprets and enriches one. This section summarizes the four methodologies the book covers as worked examples.
- [Methodology comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/comparison.md): Side-by-side decision aid based on the four methodologies the book covers. None is universally right; pick by context. Use PMBOK 7's principles + performance domains across whichever you choose.
- [DSDM®](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/dsdm.md): TL;DR: First-generation Agile method designed from scratch for large, multi-team IT development. Structurally inspired by PRINCE2. Centralized PM, timeboxed delivery, MoSCoW prioritization.
- [P3.express](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/p3-express.md): TL;DR: Open, minimalist, general-purpose method with monthly + weekly + daily cycles. Use as-is, tailor gradually. Out of scope: micro-projects (use micro.P3.express) and mega-projects (heavy tailoring or another…
- [PRINCE2®](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/prince2.md): TL;DR: Sophisticated, maximalist, general-purpose, predictive methodology. Out-of-the-box defaults target large/super-large projects; must be tailored before use. Three management layers, seven principles, two-stage…
- [Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/methodologies/scrum.md): TL;DR: First-generation Agile framework for IT development, single team ≤10 people. Decentralized PM (no PM role). Covers a subset of project management — no initiation, no closure. Lightweight; very heavy tailoring is…

### Distilled — Scrum Guide 2020

- [Scrum Guide 2020 (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/index.md): Cards distilled from the canonical 2020 Scrum Guide by Ken Schwaber and Jeff Sutherland, focused on what an agent or practitioner needs: prescriptions, accountabilities, anti-patterns and cross-refs. The Scrum Guide…
- [Scrum definition](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/01-scrum-definition.md): TL;DR: Scrum is a lightweight framework for delivering value on complex problems through small cross-functional teams, iterative Sprints, and continuous inspection-and-adaptation. Purposefully incomplete — defines just…
- [Scrum theory and the three pillars](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/02-theory-and-pillars.md): TL;DR: Scrum is grounded in empiricism (knowledge from experience and observation) and lean thinking (reduce waste, keep the essentials). Empiricism is operationalized through three pillars — Transparency, Inspection,…
- [Scrum Values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/03-scrum-values.md): TL;DR: Five values that make Scrum's empiricism work in practice: Commitment · Focus · Openness · Respect · Courage. Without them the three pillars collapse and Scrum becomes ceremony.
- [Scrum Team — structure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/04-scrum-team.md): TL;DR: A single small team (≤10), three accountabilities (Developers, Product Owner, Scrum Master), no sub-teams or hierarchies, focused on one Product Goal at a time. Cross-functional and self-managing; the entire…
- [Developers (Scrum role)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/05-developers.md): TL;DR: Anyone in the Scrum Team committed to creating any aspect of a usable Increment each Sprint. Inclusive label — "Developer" is not limited to programmers.
- [Product Owner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/06-product-owner.md): TL;DR: The Product Owner is accountable for maximizing product value and for effective Product Backlog management. One person, not a committee. May delegate, but accountability stays.
- [Scrum Master](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/07-scrum-master.md): TL;DR: The Scrum Master is accountable for establishing Scrum as defined in the Guide and for the Scrum Team's effectiveness. A true leader who serves — the team, the Product Owner, and the wider organization.
- [Scrum events — overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/08-events-overview.md): TL;DR: Five events. The Sprint is a container for the other four (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective). Each event is a formal opportunity to inspect and adapt an artifact. Skipping or…
- [The Sprint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/09-the-sprint.md): TL;DR: Fixed-length timebox of one month or less that contains all Scrum work. Heartbeat of Scrum — turns ideas into value. New Sprint starts immediately after the previous one ends.
- [Sprint Planning](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/10-sprint-planning.md): TL;DR: Initiates the Sprint by laying out the work. Whole-team collaboration. Output = Sprint Goal + Sprint Backlog. Timebox 8 h for a 1-month Sprint.
- [Daily Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/11-daily-scrum.md): TL;DR: 15-minute event for Developers. Inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal; produce an actionable plan for the next day.
- [Sprint Review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/12-sprint-review.md): TL;DR: Inspect the outcome of the Sprint with stakeholders and decide what to do next. Working session, not a demo. Timebox 4 h for a one-month Sprint.
- [Sprint Retrospective](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/13-sprint-retrospective.md): TL;DR: Plan ways to increase quality and effectiveness. Final event of the Sprint. Timebox 3 h for a one-month Sprint.
- [Product Backlog (+ Product Goal)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/14-product-backlog.md): TL;DR: Single source of work undertaken by the Scrum Team — emergent, ordered list. Its commitment is the Product Goal: the long-term objective the team plans against.
- [Sprint Backlog (+ Sprint Goal)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/15-sprint-backlog.md): TL;DR: A plan by and for the Developers. Equation: Sprint Goal (why) + selected Product Backlog items (what) + plan to deliver (how). Its commitment is the Sprint Goal.
- [Increment (+ Definition of Done)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide/16-increment.md): TL;DR: A concrete, usable, additive stepping stone toward the Product Goal. Its commitment is the Definition of Done: the formal description of "Done" quality. Multiple Increments may be created within a Sprint and…

### Distilled — Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1)

- [Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1) — distilled](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/index.md): Cards distilled from the Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1) by Ralph Jocham, John Coleman and Jeff Sutherland — an explicit adaptation of the 2020 Scrum Guide that adds theory, expands roles, and refines artifacts…
- [Purpose and key departures from SG2020](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/01-purpose-and-departures.md): TL;DR: The Expansion Pack (SGEP) is not the immutable Scrum Guide. It explains the what and why "for the current times", evolves regularly, and explicitly departs from SG2020 in several places — those departures are…
- [Foundational theory (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/02-foundational-theory.md): TL;DR: SGE explicitly grounds Scrum in seven theoretical foundations: complexity, emergence, self-managing teams, professionalism, lean thinking, empiricism, cadence. SG2020 only named empiricism + lean.
- [Additional theory (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/03-additional-theory.md): TL;DR: SGE adds six more theoretical lenses beyond the foundational seven: product thinking, systems thinking, discovery, leadership, first-principles thinking, people and change. Together with the foundational theory…
- [Stakeholder (SGE — 4th role)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/04-stakeholder.md): TL;DR: SGE elevates Stakeholder to a named role alongside PO, Product Developer, and Scrum Master. Stakeholders are not in the Scrum Team and have no accountabilities, but their classification, engagement, and…
- [Supporter (SGE — new concept)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/05-supporter.md): TL;DR: Supporter is a Stakeholder subtype — a change agent who actively makes the organization coherent with Scrum. SGE-specific concept, not present in SG2020.
- [Artificial Intelligence (SGE — new participant)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/06-artificial-intelligence.md): TL;DR: AI may be a Product Developer or a tool, but human accountability stays. Use the "human in the loop" model. AI supports Scrum's empirical process — does not override it or ethical decision-making.
- [Product Developer (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/07-product-developer.md): TL;DR: SGE-equivalent of SG2020 "Developers". May be human or automated. At least one Product Developer must be human. Accountabilities are extended.
- [Product Owner — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/08-product-owner.md): TL;DR: SGE keeps SG2020 PO accountabilities and extends substantially: PO is a leader for the Product, must be human, maximizes long-term value, owns the Definition of Outcome Done, and trusts Product Developers rather…
- [Scrum Master — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/09-scrum-master.md): TL;DR: SGE recasts the Scrum Master as a change agent at all organizational levels who is accountable for the effectiveness of the Scrum Team, Stakeholders, Supporters and affected people. Must be human. Anti-pattern…
- [Product (artifact) and Product Vision (SGE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/10-product-and-vision.md): TL;DR: SGE introduces Product as a separate artifact distinct from the Increment, and adds Product Vision as an optional but supported long-term concept. SG2020 had neither explicitly.
- [Definition of Outcome Done (SGE — new commitment)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/11-definition-of-outcome-done.md): TL;DR: New commitment in SGE, attached to the Product (not the Increment). Describes observable evidence measures that demonstrate realized benefits = "value validation". Pairs with the renamed Definition of Output Done.
- [Definition of Output Done (renamed DoD)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/12-definition-of-output-done.md): TL;DR: SGE renames the SG2020 Definition of Done to Definition of Output Done to disambiguate from outcome (the new Definition of Outcome Done). Attached to the Increment. Quality measures expressing due diligence…
- [Product Backlog extensions (PBI, Acceptance/Outcome Criteria, Refinement)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/13-product-backlog-extensions.md): TL;DR: SGE keeps the SG2020 Product Backlog basics and adds explicit treatment of Product Backlog Item (PBI), Acceptance Criteria + Outcome Criteria, Refinement (which can be a formal event), and richer Product Goal…
- [Sprint Backlog and Sprint Goal — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/14-sprint-backlog-extensions.md): TL;DR: Sprint Backlog still by-and-for the Product Developers. Major change: the Sprint Goal commitment is now owned by the whole Scrum Team (SG2020 had "Developers"). SGE re-introduces the "start with one or two PBIs"…
- [Events — SGE extensions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/scrum-guide-expanded/15-events-extensions.md): TL;DR: SGE preserves the five SG2020 events. The notable extensions: Refinement may become a formal event, Sprint Planning's three sub-topics are explicitly framed as Why / What / How, and event semantics are extended…

### Distilled — DORA Metrics in Jira

- [DORA Metrics in Jira (distilled)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/index.md): Cards distilled from a self-authored methodology document on applying DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) metrics in Jira + GitHub stacks. Practical, opinionated, end-to-end: methodology → ticket discipline →…
- [DORA fundamentals](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/01-dora-fundamentals.md): TL;DR: DORA defines four metrics correlating with org-level software performance. Read them together — Velocity ones (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time) without Stability ones (CFR, MTTR) are misleading. Out-of-the-box…
- [Ticket discipline (developer + tech lead + DoD)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/02-ticket-discipline.md): TL;DR: Discipline is the prerequisite for trustworthy DORA metrics. Four general principles, strict naming/sizing for developers, oversight rules for tech leads, and a production- gated Definition of Done.
- [GitHub-side enforcement (integration, hooks, Actions, OSS tools)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/03-github-side.md): TL;DR: Three maturity levels — basic integration → local hooks → server-side GitHub Actions. Hooks alone are bypassable; GitHub Actions are the authoritative gate. For most teams, environment: production in a GitHub…
- [Jira setup checklist](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/04-jira-setup-checklist.md): TL;DR: End-to-end actionable checklist for getting DORA metrics working in Jira Premium with GitHub. Ten sections from administrative setup through ongoing monitoring. Verification expected 2-3 weeks after setup.
- [Anti-patterns and common pitfalls](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/dora-metrics-jira/05-anti-patterns.md): TL;DR: Diagnostic table mapping symptom → likely cause → solution for the most common DORA-in-Jira failures. Plus the cross-cutting "do-nots" extracted from developer and tech-lead discipline.

### Distilled — Synthesis (cross-source)

- [Synthesis cards (cross-source)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/index.md): These cards combine material from multiple sources in this repository. Where individual per-source cards answer "what does framework X say about Y?", synthesis cards answer *"what does the whole corpus say about Y, and…
- [Done", quality, and peer review across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/01-done-and-quality.md): TL;DR: Every framework defines "done" as a commitment that prevents premature closure. They agree on the principle (built-in, not inspected; enforced by visible criteria; evolves over time) but differ in what level the…
- [Stakeholders and supporters across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/02-stakeholders-cross-source.md): TL;DR: All frameworks agree a stakeholder is anyone with interest who can impact the project (not just the customer). They diverge on structure: PMBOK 7 treats stakeholders as a performance domain with a 5-step…
- [Output vs Outcome and value validation across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/03-outcome-vs-output.md): TL;DR: Output = what the project builds. Outcome = what changes in the world for stakeholders. Most failures look like "successful delivery" while the outcome doesn't materialize. PMBOK 7 introduces the distinction;…
- [Measurement, metrics, and Parkinson's law across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/04-measurement-and-metrics.md): TL;DR: Two laws govern all metrics work: (1) what you measure becomes the goal (Parkinson / Goodhart), and (2) metrics must be read together, not in isolation (DORA). Forecast to completion is the only effective…
- [Risk, uncertainty, incidents, and stability across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/05-risk-and-stability.md): TL;DR: All frameworks treat risk as uncertainty that can impact the project (positive or negative). They differ on operationalization: PMBOK 7 prescribes layered cost-justified responses; SGE adds proactive risk…
- [Tailoring and methodology selection across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/06-tailoring-and-method-selection.md): TL;DR: Every framework explicitly addresses tailoring, and they converge on don't cherry-pick ("Frankenstein"). They diverge on when to tailor (PRINCE2: upfront required; P3.express: gradual only) and on immutability…
- [Roles and accountabilities across frameworks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/distilled/synthesis/07-roles-across-frameworks.md): TL;DR: Same project responsibilities are split across wildly different role names across frameworks. The mapping table below collapses the noise. Three universal patterns: a business authority (Sponsor/Executive/PO), a…

## Tier 3 — Full original text (per source / chapter)

### Source: DORA Metrics in Jira: Methodology, Rules, and Setup

#### (landing)

- [DORA Metrics in Jira: Methodology, Rules, and Setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/index.md): Self-authored methodology document on using DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) metrics in Jira for teams on the Jira + GitHub stack. Covers methodology, ticket discipline, GitHub-side enforcement, and Jira setup.

#### 1. Introduction: Methodology and Indicators

- [1. Introduction: Methodology and Indicators](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/01-1-introduction-methodology-and-indicators.md): DORA (DevOps Research and Assessment) is a Google Cloud research group that defined four key metrics correlating with overall organizational performance in software engineering. These metrics have become the industry…

#### 2. Ticket Discipline Rules

- [2. Ticket Discipline Rules](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/02-2-ticket-discipline-rules.md): 2. Size matters. Ten half-day tickets beat one week-long ticket. 3. A ticket is alive until the code is in production. Don't close before actual deployment. 4. Workflow needs automation — manual status drag-and-drop…

#### 3. Managing Metrics from the GitHub Side

- [3. Managing Metrics from the GitHub Side](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/03-3-managing-metrics-from-the-github-side.md): Three levels of maturity, from minimal to full control.

#### 4. Jira Setup Checklist

- [4. Jira Setup Checklist](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/04-4-jira-setup-checklist.md): Create the following automation rules (Project settings → Automation):

#### Appendix: Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them

- [Appendix: Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/dora-metrics-jira/05-appendix-common-pitfalls-and-how-to-avoid-them.md): ---

### Source: The 2020 Scrum Guide

#### (landing)

- [The 2020 Scrum Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/index.md): Mirror of the 2020 Scrum Guide by Ken Schwaber and Jeff Sutherland, split into chunks for use as a knowledge base for agents.

#### Purpose of the Scrum Guide

- [Purpose of the Scrum Guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/01-purpose-of-the-scrum-guide.md): We developed Scrum in the early 1990s. We wrote the first version of the Scrum Guide in 2010 to help people worldwide understand Scrum. We have evolved the Guide since then through small, functional updates. Together,…

#### Scrum Definition

- [Scrum Definition](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/02-scrum-definition.md): Scrum is a lightweight framework that helps people, teams and organizations generate value through adaptive solutions for complex problems.

#### Scrum Theory

- [Scrum Theory](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/03-scrum-theory/index.md): Scrum is founded on empiricism and lean thinking. Empiricism asserts that knowledge comes from experience and making decisions based on what is observed. Lean thinking reduces waste and focuses on the essentials.
- [Transparency](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/03-scrum-theory/01-transparency.md): The emergent process and work must be visible to those performing the work as well as those receiving the work. With Scrum, important decisions are based on the perceived state of its three formal artifacts. Artifacts…
- [Inspection](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/03-scrum-theory/02-inspection.md): The Scrum artifacts and the progress toward agreed goals must be inspected frequently and diligently to detect potentially undesirable variances or problems. To help with inspection, Scrum provides cadence in the form…
- [Adaptation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/03-scrum-theory/03-adaptation.md): If any aspects of a process deviate outside acceptable limits or if the resulting product is unacceptable, the process being applied or the materials being produced must be adjusted. The adjustment must be made as soon…

#### Scrum Values

- [Scrum Values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/04-scrum-values.md): Successful use of Scrum depends on people becoming more proficient in living five values:

#### Scrum Team

- [Scrum Team](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/05-scrum-team/index.md): The fundamental unit of Scrum is a small team of people, a Scrum Team. The Scrum Team consists of one Scrum Master, one Product Owner, and Developers. Within a Scrum Team, there are no sub-teams or hierarchies. It is a…
- [Developers](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/05-scrum-team/01-developers.md): Developers are the people in the Scrum Team that are committed to creating any aspect of a usable Increment each Sprint.
- [Product Owner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/05-scrum-team/02-product-owner.md): The Product Owner is accountable for maximizing the value of the product resulting from the work of the Scrum Team. How this is done may vary widely across organizations, Scrum Teams, and individuals.
- [Scrum Master](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/05-scrum-team/03-scrum-master.md): The Scrum Master is accountable for establishing Scrum as defined in the Scrum Guide. They do this by helping everyone understand Scrum theory and practice, both within the Scrum Team and the organization.

#### Scrum Events

- [Scrum Events](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/06-scrum-events/index.md): The Sprint is a container for all other events. Each event in Scrum is a formal opportunity to inspect and adapt Scrum artifacts. These events are specifically designed to enable the transparency required. Failure to…
- [The Sprint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/06-scrum-events/01-the-sprint.md): Sprints are the heartbeat of Scrum, where ideas are turned into value.
- [Sprint Planning](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/06-scrum-events/02-sprint-planning.md): Sprint Planning initiates the Sprint by laying out the work to be performed for the Sprint. This resulting plan is created by the collaborative work of the entire Scrum Team.
- [Daily Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/06-scrum-events/03-daily-scrum.md): The purpose of the Daily Scrum is to inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal and adapt the Sprint Backlog as necessary, adjusting the upcoming planned work.
- [Sprint Review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/06-scrum-events/04-sprint-review.md): The purpose of the Sprint Review is to inspect the outcome of the Sprint and determine future adaptations. The Scrum Team presents the results of their work to key stakeholders and progress toward the Product Goal is…
- [Sprint Retrospective](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/06-scrum-events/05-sprint-retrospective.md): The purpose of the Sprint Retrospective is to plan ways to increase quality and effectiveness.

#### Scrum Artifacts

- [Scrum Artifacts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/07-scrum-artifacts/index.md): Scrum’s artifacts represent work or value. They are designed to maximize transparency of key information. Thus, everyone inspecting them has the same basis for adaptation.
- [Product Backlog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/07-scrum-artifacts/01-product-backlog.md): The Product Backlog is an emergent, ordered list of what is needed to improve the product. It is the single source of work undertaken by the Scrum Team.
- [Sprint Backlog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/07-scrum-artifacts/02-sprint-backlog.md): The Sprint Backlog is composed of the Sprint Goal (why), the set of Product Backlog items selected for the Sprint (what), as well as an actionable plan for delivering the Increment (how).
- [Increment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/07-scrum-artifacts/03-increment.md): An Increment is a concrete stepping stone toward the Product Goal. Each Increment is additive to all prior Increments and thoroughly verified, ensuring that all Increments work together. In order to provide value, the…

#### End Note

- [End Note](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide/08-end-note.md): Scrum is free and offered in this Guide. The Scrum framework, as outlined herein, is immutable. While implementing only parts of Scrum is possible, the result is not Scrum. Scrum exists only in its entirety and…

### Source: Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1)

#### (landing)

- [Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/index.md): Mirror of the Scrum Guide Expansion Pack (v2026.1) by Ralph Jocham, John Coleman and Jeff Sutherland — an adaptation of the original 2020 Scrum Guide.

#### Background

- [Background](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/01-background.md): Jeff Sutherland’s 1993 Scrum adoption at Easel was inspired by Christopher Langton’s papers [1-2] on Complex Adaptive Systems (CAS) [3-6] theory from Los Alamos Labs, which showed that systems evolve quicker at the…

#### Purpose of the Scrum Guide Expansion Pack

- [Purpose of the Scrum Guide Expansion Pack](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/02-purpose-of-the-scrum-guide-expansion-pack.md): This Scrum Guide Expansion Pack(SGEP) explains the what and why of each element of Scrum for the current times. Each element serves a specific purpose and contributes to the overall value and results realized with…

#### Scrum in a Nutshell

- [Scrum in a Nutshell](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/03-scrum-in-a-nutshell.md): Scrum is a framework for complex [12-17] Product delivery, where expertise is valuable but more than expertise is needed, and cause and effect are only coherent in retrospect. Scrum addresses the full Product…

#### Supporting and Complementary Theory

- [Supporting and Complementary Theory](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/index.md): Scrum is founded on a self-managing Scrum Team [11], emergence, empiricism [19], and lean thinking [20]. It is underpinned by the supporting and complementary theory below and ideas such as:
- [Complexity–The Case for Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/01-complexity-the-case-for-scrum.md): For complex work, like building Products, there are more unknowns than knowns, expertise alone is valuable but insufficient, and cause and effect are only coherent in retrospect. Complexity thinking [12-17] provides…
- [Emergence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/02-emergence.md): Emergence [21] is when meaningful patterns or behaviors arise from interactions within complex [12-17] systems-patterns you can’t predict just by looking at the parts alone. In Scrum, emergence isn’t tightly controlled…
- [Self-Managing Scrum Team](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/03-self-managing-scrum-team.md): A self-managing [11] Scrum Team checks whether they are on track, takes action when not on track, decides how to work, resolves Scrum Team conflict, and fixes problems in the Scrum Team. This means that, generally,…
- [Professionalism](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/04-professionalism.md): Professionalism is about striving for excellence and working together to deliver value in a respectful, transparent, and accountable way. Being professional means that one will always do certain things and others…
- [Lean Thinking](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/05-lean-thinking.md): ‘Lean thinking [20] reduces waste in the work and how it is carried out, and focuses on the flow of value and continuous improvement.’ The Lean principles are built on continuous improvement and respect for people. By…
- [Empiricism](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/06-empiricism.md): Empiricism [19] is the principle of making decisions informed by objective or observable evidence in learning cycles, often exploratory. It can be useful in situations where more than expertise is needed. Scrum is…
- [Cadence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/04-supporting-and-complementary-theory/07-cadence.md): Working in Sprints provides a consistent rhythm that helps the Scrum Team focus on clear, short-term goals. This cadence supports regular inspection and adaptation, enabling the Scrum Team to learn and adjust informed…

#### The Three Pillars of Scrum’s Empirical Process Control

- [The Three Pillars of Scrum’s Empirical Process Control](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/05-the-three-pillars-of-scrum-s-empirical-process-control/index.md): Empiricism, at its core, is the philosophy that knowledge comes from experience and observation. Valuable insights emerge from curiosity, experience, experimentation, data, visualization, and observation. Empirical…
- [Transparency](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/05-the-three-pillars-of-scrum-s-empirical-process-control/01-transparency.md): Transparency is a pillar of Scrum. It reveals reality and work clarity, and enables empiricism. Transparency reveals a more accurate perception of reality and is the entry point for Inspection and Adaptation. The…
- [Inspection](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/05-the-three-pillars-of-scrum-s-empirical-process-control/02-inspection.md): Inspection is a pillar of Scrum. Inspection is looking at reality, given the direction of the Product (the Product Goal) and the effectiveness of the Scrum Team and Stakeholders. Inspection enables Adaptation.…
- [Adaptation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/05-the-three-pillars-of-scrum-s-empirical-process-control/03-adaptation.md): Adaptation is a pillar of Scrum. Given the direction of the Product, the Scrum Team and Stakeholders are expected to adapt to reality the moment improvement opportunities emerge, such as experiment outcomes, insights,…

#### The Scrum Values

- [The Scrum Values](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/06-the-scrum-values.md): The Scrum Values —focus, openness, commitment, courage, and \respect—\help create a Scrum Team environment that supports psychological safety and positive collaboration, which align with principles identified in…

#### More Supporting and Complementary Theory

- [More Supporting and Complementary Theory](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/index.md): top
- [Product Thinking](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/01-product-thinking.md): People consume Products (including services), not projects. A Product is the conduit to deliver value, balancing the short- and long-term. This is why Scrum has a Product Owner and not a Project Owner. Products are…
- [Systems Thinking](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/02-systems-thinking.md): Systems thinking [36] acknowledges the interconnectedness of elements within organizational and social contexts, recognizing that actions in one area ripple in ways that aren’t always predictable or linear.…
- [Discovery](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/03-discovery.md): Discovery [38-39] often starts with understanding people’s expectations, needs, and wants through observation, analysis, conversations, and synthesis toward a desired outcome. Once a Scrum Team has gathered insights,…
- [Leadership](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/04-leadership.md): Leadership is the ability to influence, guide, and inspire a group of people to achieve a common goal while avoiding demotivation. It inspires thoughts, actions, and passion and fosters clear strategic directions. It…
- [First Principles Thinking](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/05-first-principles-thinking.md): First principles thinking is a method of problem-solving that involves breaking down challenges into their most fundamental truths and discovering solutions from the ground up. Instead of relying on analogy or…
- [People and Change](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/07-more-supporting-and-complementary-theory/06-people-and-change.md): The level of difficulty in adopting Scrum should not be underestimated. Scrum offers some guiding principles through its elements. It offers an approach to go back to first principles.

#### The Scrum Roles in the Expansion Pack

- [The Scrum Roles in the Expansion Pack](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/index.md): An accountability is a list of expectations that one is accountable for, e.g., the Product Owner accountable for value, the Scrum Master for the Scrum Team’s effectiveness, and Product Developers for creating the…
- [Stakeholder](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/01-stakeholder.md): Stakeholder is a role. A Stakeholder is an entity, individual, or group interested in, affected by, or impacting inputs, activities, and outcomes. Stakeholders have a direct or indirect interest inside or outside the…
- [Supporter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/02-supporter.md): Supporter is a specific Stakeholder type. Supporters are supporting Stakeholders and change agents. Supporters are often part of a powerful guiding coalition [45], who inspire and remove demotivating factors.…
- [Artificial Intelligence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/03-artificial-intelligence.md): Artificial Intelligence (AI) is increasingly part of the work environment and may significantly expand a Scrum Team’s capabilities in discovery, decision-making, Product development, and value realization.
- [Product Developer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/04-product-developer.md): Product Developer is a role and an accountability. All Product Developers together should possess all the skills needed to create Increments. The combined skill set is often referred to as cross-functional.
- [Product Owner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/05-product-owner.md): Product Owner is a role and an accountability. The Product Owner must be human. To be effective, the Product Owner should be a leader for the Product. The Product Owner maximizes long-term value and needs to know where…
- [Scrum Master](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/08-the-scrum-roles-in-the-expansion-pack/06-scrum-master.md): The Scrum Master is a role and an accountability. The Scrum Master must be human. The Scrum Master is a change agent who works at all organizational levels and across business areas. The Scrum Master leads by example…

#### The Scrum Artifacts in the Expansion Pack

- [The Scrum Artifacts in the Expansion Pack](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/index.md): Scrum’s artifacts provide Transparency about what the Scrum Team and Stakeholders believe will deliver value. Thus, everyone can have the same basis for Inspection and Adaptation.
- [Product](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/01-product.md): The Product is an artifact. A Product can be a holistic experience or a platform. It can also be a service, physical, digital, or hybrid, delivering continuous value to Stakeholders (including but not limited to users).
- [Commitment: Definition of Outcome Done](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/02-commitment-definition-of-outcome-done.md): The Definition of Outcome Done is a commitment. It describes the observable evidence measures (quantitative or qualitative) required to demonstrate realized benefits, often referred to as value validation. It could be…
- [Increment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/03-increment.md): The Increment is an artifact. It is the integration of the work completed to the standard of the Definition of Output Done. The Increment is an output and a Product candidate.
- [Commitment: Definition of Output Done](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/04-commitment-definition-of-output-done.md): The Definition of Output Done is a commitment. It formally describes the quality measures that express due diligence for the Increment so that it could be delivered to Stakeholders.
- [Product Backlog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/05-product-backlog.md): The Product Backlog is an artifact. It is the emergent, ordered (sequenced) list of Product Backlog Items needed to attain the Product Goal. The Product Backlog provides Transparency (work clarity) and is the single…
- [Product Backlog Item](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/06-product-backlog-item.md): A Product Backlog Item is a potentially valuable item in the Product Backlog. It is not necessarily in any specific format. It is intended to deal with a problem or opportunity. It can include Acceptance Criteria that…
- [Acceptance Criteria](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/07-acceptance-criteria.md): Acceptance Criteria, if they exist, describe when an output for a specific Product Backlog Item is complete, in addition to the Definition of Output Done. Acceptance Criteria in refined items should provide unambiguous…
- [Refinement](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/08-refinement.md): Refinement is an activity. It may be formal (an additional event) (in the SGEP it is a key element) or informal. Refinement is an ongoing emergent process that fosters clarity and reduces risk; it builds enough…
- [Commitment: Product Goal](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/09-commitment-product-goal.md): The Product Goal is a commitment. It is represented through the Product Backlog, which is owned by the Product Owner. It is the current single, more strategic, ambitious objective (the why). It gives direction for the…
- [What about a Product Vision?](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/10-what-about-a-product-vision.md): Many organizations work with a Product Vision, which helps visualize a potential future. The Scrum Team can use a Vision as input for considering a Product Goal. A Product Vision is a meaningful, long-term set of…
- [Sprint Backlog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/11-sprint-backlog.md): The Sprint Backlog is an artifact. It is composed of the Sprint Goal (the why for the Sprint), the set of Product Backlog Items selected (the what, also known as the forecast) for the Sprint, and often has an…
- [Commitment: Sprint Goal](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/09-the-scrum-artifacts-in-the-expansion-pack/12-commitment-sprint-goal.md): To attain coherence, the Sprint Goal is a commitment created and owned by the Scrum Team (SG2020 Developers owned the commitment). The Sprint Goal is the single unifying objective (coherent with the Product Goal) of…

#### The Scrum Events in the Expansion Pack

- [The Scrum Events in the Expansion Pack](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/index.md): Scrum combines four timeboxed events for Inspection and Adaptation within a containing fifth event of determinate consistent length, the Sprint. These events support the Scrum pillars of Transparency, Inspection, and…
- [The Sprint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/01-the-sprint.md): The Sprint is an event where ideas are turned into value. The Sprint is the container event. It is an iteration of a determinate time in which work is carried out. It provides Focus and stability. A Sprint is no longer…
- [Sprint Planning](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/02-sprint-planning.md): Sprint Planning is an event. This first event of the Sprint is where the Scrum Team gives Focus and creates commitment.
- [The *Why* for the Sprint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/03-the-why-for-the-sprint.md): The Product Owner proposes data-informed ideas (e.g., Refinement, result feedback, other learnings) for how the Product could increase its value and utility in the current Sprint. The Product Owner and the Product…
- [The *What* toward the *Why*](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/04-the-what-toward-the-why.md): Through collaboration with the Product Owner, the Product Developers select items from the Product Backlog to include in the current Sprint. The Scrum Team may refine these items, which increases understanding and…
- [The *How* for the *What*](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/05-the-how-for-the-what.md): How the work is done is at the sole discretion of the Product Developers. No one else tells the Product Developers how to do their work. The Product Developers select their own work; no one else assigns or pushes…
- [Daily Scrum](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/06-daily-scrum.md): The Daily Scrum is an event. At the Daily Scrum, the Product Developers collaborate on progress toward the Sprint Goal and update the actionable plan, the Sprint Backlog, until the next Daily Scrum. In the event the…
- [Sprint Review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/07-sprint-review.md): The Sprint Review is an event. It is an interactive, collaborative working session. Often, the Scrum Team shares the current Product Goal and presents the Definition of Output Done and the Definition of Outcome Done to…
- [Sprint Retrospective](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/10-the-scrum-events-in-the-expansion-pack/08-sprint-retrospective.md): The Sprint Retrospective is an event. At this event, the Scrum Team agrees on how to improve. Bad assumptions are also explored, i.e., assumptions that led the Scrum Team in the wrong direction. Good things like…

#### End Note

- [End Note](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OpenMindUA/tech-lead-notes/main/docs/sources/scrum-guide-expanded/11-end-note.md): Scrum is like a mirror. If the image in the mirror is not as expected, should the mirror be hidden?
